master_row.title: Trawinistic Winding Number
master_row.canonical_statement: The Trawinistic winding number counts the net phase accumulation around a closed loop encircling the zero point. Because the local loop class is lemniscatic rather than simply circular, the winding may take half-integer values as well as integer values.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a contour integral relating π, Γ, ∇, θ, ell.
master_row.dictionary: For a closed loop () around the TZP, the Trawinistic winding number (w) is defined as (w=12_ ), where () is the phase acquired by a field encircling the TZP.
master_row.encyclopedia: This equation defines a layered Lagrangian architecture: gravity, quantum fields, topology, information, and biological organization are treated as coupled sectors of one Trawinistic action density. Interpretive role: This quantity is the canonical topological counter for later charge, holonomy, and causal-loop invariants. It also makes the half-branch structure of the Trawin loop geometry numerically visible.
